title: Ubidots API secrets should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Ubidots API credential detected in assignment. This pattern identifies Ubidots API tokens when assigned to variables or used in configuration settings. Ubidots tokens provide comprehensive access to IoT data collection, device management, dashboards, events, and analytics within the Ubidots IoT platform. When these credentials are exposed in source code, attackers can gain unauthorized control over your entire IoT infrastructure to access all device data, sensor readings, historical analytics, and operational metrics, create or manipulate devices to inject false data or disrupt monitoring systems, access and modify dashboards revealing business intelligence and operational insights, configure malicious events and alerts to mask suspicious activity or create false notifications, steal comprehensive datasets for competitive intelligence or unauthorized resale, access user management functions to add unauthorized accounts or modify permissions, manipulate variable configurations to redirect data flows or corrupt data integrity, access billing information and potentially abuse premium features leading to unexpected charges, leverage the platform's reputation to conduct fraudulent IoT activities, and export all collected data for analysis of business operations and patterns. The business impact can be devastating including massive data breaches exposing sensor data and operational intelligence with resulting regulatory fines under IoT and privacy regulations, severe reputational damage from compromised IoT security and data integrity, operational disruption from manipulated or corrupted device data affecting business decisions, financial losses from premium feature abuse and potential legal liabilities, loss of competitive advantage through stolen operational data and business intelligence, compliance violations requiring extensive reporting and remediation efforts, customer trust erosion from security breaches affecting IoT deployments, and exposure of confidential business processes and operational strategies. Respond immediately by logging into your Ubidots account dashboard, navigating to API credentials section, identifying and revoking the compromised token immediately, generating a new API token with strictly minimal required permissions, updating all applications, devices, scripts, and integration systems with the new credentials, conducting a thorough review of account audit logs to identify all unauthorized activities during the exposure timeframe, scanning all devices and variables for unauthorized modifications or suspicious data patterns, verifying user lists and removing any unauthorized accounts, examining event configurations and disabling any suspicious or unauthorized triggers, analyzing data export logs for signs of unauthorized access or bulk data theft, reviewing billing statements for unexpected premium usage or charges, implementing stronger authentication controls including two-factor authentication where available, configuring IP address restrictions and access controls to limit token usage, migrating credential storage to secure environment variables or dedicated secret management systems, establishing comprehensive monitoring and alerting for unusual API usage patterns, implementing regular token rotation schedules as a proactive security measure, conducting security training for team members on proper IoT credential handling, documenting the incident thoroughly for compliance reporting and security audit purposes, and notifying affected customers and regulatory authorities if IoT data was potentially compromised during the breach window.

Ubidots API token detected. Ubidots API tokens provide programmatic access to the Ubidots IoT platform, enabling comprehensive control over devices, data collection, dashboards, analytics, and user management. These tokens can access all device data and sensor readings from your IoT infrastructure, create, modify, and delete devices within your IoT deployment, retrieve historical data and analytics for business intelligence purposes, access and modify dashboard configurations revealing operational insights, configure events and alerts that could mask malicious activities, manage user accounts and permissions within your organization, export large datasets containing sensitive operational and business data, manipulate data variables to inject false information or corrupt data integrity, access billing and subscription information potentially leading to service abuse, and leverage platform integrations to access connected third-party services. When compromised, attackers can steal comprehensive IoT data containing operational metrics, sensor readings, environmental data, and business intelligence, manipulate device configurations to disrupt operations or inject false data into your systems, create unauthorized devices that could be used for surveillance or data collection activities, delete critical devices and historical data causing operational disruptions and data loss, access dashboard analytics to understand business operations and competitive intelligence, configure malicious alerts to hide suspicious activities or create false emergency notifications, steal user credentials and account information for further system compromises, export datasets for resale or competitive intelligence purposes, manipulate data flows to redirect information to attacker-controlled systems, and abuse premium features leading to unexpected billing charges and service limitations. This can result in severe security and business consequences including massive operational data breaches exposing sensitive IoT infrastructure information, regulatory compliance violations under IoT security and data protection frameworks, significant financial losses from service abuse and potential legal liabilities, reputational damage from compromised IoT security affecting customer trust, loss of competitive advantage through stolen operational data and business intelligence, operational disruption from manipulated or corrupted device data affecting business decisions, privacy violations if personal data is collected through IoT devices, and exposure of confidential business processes and operational strategies through analytics access. Take immediate action by accessing your Ubidots account management dashboard, navigating to the API tokens or credentials section, identifying and immediately revoking the compromised token, generating a new API token with minimal necessary permissions and restricted scopes, updating all devices, applications, integrations, and automated systems with the new credentials, conducting a comprehensive audit of account logs to identify unauthorized activities during the exposure period, reviewing all devices and data variables for unauthorized modifications or suspicious patterns, checking dashboard configurations and analytics for any unauthorized changes, examining event and alert configurations for malicious modifications, analyzing data export logs for signs of unauthorized data access or theft, reviewing user account lists and removing any unauthorized or suspicious accounts, implementing two-factor authentication and additional security measures where available, configuring IP address restrictions and access controls to limit token usage to authorized systems, storing new credentials securely in environment variables or dedicated secret management systems, establishing continuous monitoring and alerting for unusual API usage patterns and access attempts, implementing regular token rotation schedules as part of ongoing security hygiene, providing security awareness training for team members handling IoT credentials, documenting this security incident for compliance and audit purposes, and notifying relevant stakeholders and regulatory authorities if sensitive data was potentially accessed or compromised.

Ubidots authentication token detected in header configuration. Ubidots uses the X-AUTH-TOKEN header for API authentication, providing access to comprehensive IoT platform capabilities including device management, data collection, analytics, and user administration. These tokens enable full control over IoT infrastructure and can access all device data and sensor readings from connected IoT devices, create, modify, and delete devices within the platform ecosystem, retrieve comprehensive historical data and analytics for operational intelligence, access and configure dashboards revealing critical business and operational metrics, manage events, alerts, and notification systems that could be manipulated for malicious purposes, administer user accounts and permissions potentially compromising organizational security, export extensive datasets containing sensitive operational and competitive intelligence, manipulate data variables and device configurations affecting data integrity, access billing and subscription details enabling potential service abuse, and integrate with third-party services expanding the attack surface. When an X-AUTH-TOKEN is compromised, attackers can conduct comprehensive IoT infrastructure reconnaissance and data theft, manipulate device operations to disrupt business processes or inject false data, create unauthorized monitoring devices for surveillance purposes, delete critical operational data and device configurations, access real-time operational dashboards to understand business processes and vulnerabilities, configure malicious alerts to mask attack activities or create false emergencies, compromise user accounts to establish persistent access, steal comprehensive datasets for competitive intelligence or unauthorized commercial use, manipulate data flows to exfiltrate information or redirect operations, and abuse premium platform features leading to service disruptions and financial losses. The security and business impact can be catastrophic including massive breaches of IoT operational data exposing critical business intelligence, severe regulatory compliance violations under IoT security frameworks and data protection laws, significant financial losses from service abuse and potential legal liabilities, devastating reputational damage from compromised IoT security affecting customer confidence, loss of competitive advantage through stolen operational metrics and business processes, operational disruption from corrupted device data affecting critical business decisions, privacy violations if IoT devices collect personal or sensitive information, and complete exposure of business operations and strategic planning through analytics access. Execute immediate containment and recovery by accessing your Ubidots account security settings, navigating to API token management, immediately revoking the compromised X-AUTH-TOKEN, generating a new authentication token with minimal required permissions and restricted access scopes, updating all applications, devices, infrastructure components, and integration systems with the new token, conducting exhaustive audit log analysis to identify all unauthorized activities and data access during the compromise period, systematically reviewing all devices and data configurations for unauthorized modifications or malicious implants, examining dashboard and analytics access logs for evidence of operational intelligence theft, checking event and alert configurations for malicious modifications that could hide future attacks, analyzing data export activities and access patterns for signs of systematic data theft, reviewing user account access and removing any unauthorized or compromised accounts, implementing multi-factor authentication and enhanced security controls across the platform, configuring strict IP address restrictions and geographical access limitations for token usage, migrating authentication credentials to secure secret management systems with proper access controls, establishing comprehensive real-time monitoring and alerting for suspicious API activity patterns, implementing automated token rotation policies as part of security hardening measures, conducting thorough security awareness training for all personnel with IoT platform access, creating detailed incident documentation for regulatory compliance and security audit purposes, and immediately notifying relevant stakeholders, customers, and regulatory authorities about potential data exposure and security implications.
